relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: John appears in the wilderness, outside the center, calling people back to truth.
His message is not mere guilt; it is a call to turn consciousness because heaven’s reign is near.
He becomes the wilderness voice preparing a straight way.
His life itself carries prophetic simplicity and wildness.
The people stream out from Jerusalem, Judea, and the Jordan region.
They enter the waters confessing misalignment and seeking cleansing.
John confronts religious power that comes without transformation.
Lineage alone cannot replace living alignment.
The root is being examined; fruitless patterns cannot remain untouched.
Water prepares, but the coming one immerses in holy Spirit and fire.
The threshing image separates what nourishes from what cannot endure.
Jesus comes to the Jordan and enters the same water as the people.
John feels the reversal: the purifier comes to be baptized.
Jesus fulfills right-alignment not by distance, but by entering the human path.
The waters open into heavens, Spirit, and dove-like descent.
The voice names him beloved Son, the one in whom the Divine delights.
